title: DSDS Foundation Definitions
description: Defines documents for foundations: the broad, cross-cutting design domains a system is built on, such as color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, and content. A foundation document holds the identity and metadata of a domain. The `metadata` object holds the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries.

foundation.description: The principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a broad design domain across the system, such as color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, accessibility, and content. Foundations are wider than a single visual attribute. A domain may be visual (color, spacing) or cross-cutting (accessibility, content). The top level holds its identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: principles, scale, motion, guidelines, accessibility, content, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a foundation.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the foundation (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape', 'accessibility', 'content'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

description.description: What this foundation covers, the design domain it governs, and its role in the system. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata: the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json).

relationships.description: Typed dependency edges from this foundation to other entities — e.g. 'depends-on' the tokens it builds on. Inverse edges are derived by tooling. Use the `links` metadata field for external resources.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this foundation, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. It accepts the foundation-specific kinds (principles, scale, motion) plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display. Put internal artifact links (such as token group references) in the `links` metadata field, not in document block entries.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only — a separate space that accepts the same block kinds as `documentBlocks`. `documentBlocks` is the default: put anything a human reader needs there. Use `agentDocumentBlocks` only for content that helps an agent build correctly but is noise for humans. For example: hard MUST/MUST-NOT rules an agent should treat as firm limits; notes that correct a mistake an agent is likely to make, such as reaching for this entity when a confusable one fits better; constraints backed by test-run evidence; and machine-checkable criteria with verification modes. A hard rule that humans also need (such as an accessibility requirement) belongs in `documentBlocks`, not here. Agent blocks add to the human docs — they never replace or duplicate them. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays, human blocks first.
